verdicts.interest_take: The episode's beating heart: a real Van Gogh letter reframes Ted's slump as a season, not a verdict. The standout card.
verdicts.writing_notes: moment/idea/why all distinct; idea is the genuine payoff; warm and unhurried; category right.

verdicts.interest_take: The setting IS the joke — Ted reinvents in Amsterdam the very system Amsterdam (Cruyff/Ajax) gave the sport. Real 'I never caught that' for a non-fan.
verdicts.writing_notes: Clean and surprising; the 5-0 Ajax loss detail lands the irony. Meta & trivia fits.

verdicts.interest_take: A precise needle-drop Easter egg (same Ricky Nelson cut as Jack Rabbit Slim's) hiding in plain sight — the kind of thing only a film buff catches.
verdicts.writing_notes: Tight; the song-match specificity earns it. Trim the 'foot-massage talk' aside if room is tight, but it's fine.

verdicts.interest_take: Deep-cut Disney 1959 homage behind the triangle hallucination — surprising, and it's doing real story work (sparks Ted's tactic).
verdicts.writing_notes: Good. Pairs naturally with Total Football without duplicating it (source vs. result).

verdicts.interest_take: The location choice carries the scene's meaning — three pink triangles that rhyme with the episode's triangle motif. Genuinely illuminating.
verdicts.writing_notes: idea teaches the Homomonument cleanly; why ties it to the motif. Strong. Relationships fits.

verdicts.interest_take: Teaches a lovely untranslatable Dutch word a non-technical reader will keep. Warm, self-contained.
verdicts.writing_notes: Clear; idea (the word) is the payoff, why grounds it emotionally. Good.

verdicts.interest_take: The long Roy/Jamie thaw paying off in the gentlest image. More emotional beat than 'I never caught that,' but a warm reader will love it.
verdicts.writing_notes: Well-written; risk is it's recap of an obvious scene rather than a hidden gem. Earns its place on heart, not surprise.

verdicts.interest_take: A private joke made public: Beard's Bowie bit is real autobiography for writer Brendan Hunt's Amsterdam years. Ties the whole show back to Amsterdam.
verdicts.writing_notes: Strong meta card; the Boom Chicago / show-origin detail is the real reward. Keep.

verdicts.interest_take: Rewards watching the side stories — Chet Baker history plus a minor character's unguarded joy. Modest but real.
verdicts.writing_notes: Fine. Sits comfortably in the back half of the set; not a headliner.

verdicts.interest_take: The sunflower = Kansas state flower is a nice double-meaning, but it heavily overlaps the Van Gogh card and the throughline card — title trivia more than fresh insight.
verdicts.writing_notes: Well-written, but as a 'title explainer' it competes with the stronger sowing-time card. Reframe to lead with the Kansas-flower fact so it earns distinct space.
verdicts.suggested_edit: Retitle to foreground the surprise — e.g. title: "The sunflower is from Kansas" — and trim the thematic overlap with the docent card so it reads as a standalone 'home meets him abroad' fact, not a second statement of the theme.

verdicts.interest_take: Real-city-vs-built-set craft (filming beside million-dollar Van Goghs) is a satisfying behind-the-scenes peek. Lower-tier but distinct.
verdicts.writing_notes: Solid production card; keep it clearly separate from the structure card by staying on locations/sets only. interest 3 is right; bottom of the set.

verdicts.interest_take: Mostly recaps what the reader just watched (lists the threads) plus 'critics loved it' — the weakest, most padding-like card in a crowded set.
verdicts.writing_notes: Honest about opinion-vs-fact, but the one-night-many-threads structure is self-evident from watching; little hidden payoff. Cut to keep the set tight.

verdicts.interest_take: Interpretive throughline that restates the Van Gogh docent card's idea across all storylines — the most redundant card; lowest grounding (3, partial) and surprise (2).
verdicts.writing_notes: Well-phrased but duplicative: its 'idea' is the same Van Gogh line the sowing-time card already carries better. Cut; let the docent card own the theme.

episode_notes: Reference-rich Amsterdam episode; the danger here is volume, not quality. 13 included cards is too many for a phone read, with three cards (van-gogh-sowing-time, sunflowers-kansas, do-what-youre-meant-to-do) all circling the same theme and two production cards (structure, locations) overlapping. Recommend a tight set of 11: keep the Van Gogh docent card as the anchor and centerpiece; CUT 'do-what-youre-meant-to-do' (restates the docent card, lowest grounding/surprise) and 'bottle-episode-structure' (recap + critics' opinion, least hidden payoff). REVISE the Kansas card to lead with the state-flower surprise so it stops echoing the theme cards. Best-first ordering leads with the three genuine 'I never caught that' winners — the Van Gogh letter, Total Football irony, and the Pulp Fiction needle-drop — then the Disney/Homomonument references, the warm gezellig/Roy-Jamie/Piggy Stardust beats, and finally the lower-surprise side-story and production cards. Spoiler note: Colin/Trent and Roy/Jamie advance multi-episode arcs (series-scope); both are still rewarding in-episode, so kept. The excluded contested card (jan-maas-cruyff-pronunciation) is correctly held — its who-corrects-whom framing is wrong and it would not have earned a slot anyway.
